title: dryopteris atrata f. cycadina
intro: Dryopteris atrata f. cycadina, also known as the Black Scaly Male Fern, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of fern, characterized by its dark green fronds and scaly rhizome. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Dryopteris atrata f. cycadina include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy immune response. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture, or added to topical creams and ointments for its potential therapeutic benefits.
